g.text: Maps and Comparators in Java Collections

g.data-narration: A Map stores key-value pairs where each key maps to exactly one value, enabling efficient data retrieval.
The Map interface provides essential methods for storing, retrieving, and manipulating key-value pairs efficiently.
HashMap is the most commonly used Map implementation offering O(1) constant-time performance for basic operations.
g.text: • One null key, multiple null values

g.data-narration: LinkedHashMap maintains insertion order through a doubly-linked list, useful when order matters.
TreeMap stores entries in sorted order using Red-Black tree, providing O(log n) performance for operations.
g.text: • Sorted by natural order or Comparator

g.data-narration: This comparison table helps choose the right Map implementation based on ordering, nulls, synchronization, and performance needs.
The Map.Entry interface represents a key-value pair, essential for iterating through map entries efficiently.
Comparators define custom ordering for objects, returning negative, zero, or positive for less than, equal, or greater than.
Three ways to create comparators: anonymous class (traditional), lambda expression (modern), and Comparator utility methods.
Advanced comparators include chaining with thenComparing, null handling with nullsFirst/nullsLast, and reversal with reversed.
g.text: // Sort by grade desc, then by age asc: Comparator.comparingInt(Student::getGrade).reversed().thenComparingInt(Student::getAge)

g.data-narration: Practical example: sorting a Map by its values using entrySet conversion and Comparator.comparingByValue.
g.text: Practical Example: Sorting Map by Values

g.data-narration: Performance varies: HashMap/LinkedHashMap offer O(1) constant time, while TreeMap provides O(log n) sorted operations.
Key pitfalls: HashMap keys need proper hashCode/equals, TreeMap requires Comparable or Comparator, comparator should match equals.
g.text: • TreeMap: keys must be Comparable or use Comparator
• Use appropriate Map for needs: HashMap (general),

g.data-narration: Remember: Map is not a Collection, HashMap allows one null key, TreeMap sorts by comparator, compare returns negative/zero/positive.
